cards.title: US Government Pulls Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails. Cybersecurity researchers have since begun investigating the vulnerability.

cards.title: Reliance Weaving AI Into Telecom Services
cards.body: Reliance is integrating AI into telecom services used by over 500 million people. Billionaire Ambani's vision aims to bring AI into every call, app, and home.

cards.title: Allbirds CEO Launches New AI Business
cards.body: The Allbirds CEO launched a startup with substantial seed funding but currently operates as a sole founder. The company's next steps remain unclear despite the major capital injection.

cards.title: ASML Disputes US Claims About China
cards.body: The US alleged that ASML's top chip tool may have ended up in China. ASML denies the claim, citing strong commercial incentives to protect its export license.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires DeductiveAI for Up to $85M
cards.body: Elastic agreed to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI, which uses AI to catch and resolve software bugs. The startup was founded just three years ago before the acquisition.

cards.title: Baseten Raising $1.5B at $13B Valuation
cards.body: AI inference startup Baseten is reportedly finalizing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation. The raise comes months after the company's previous mega-round amid the "inference gold rush."

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Unit Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is spinning off its AI video team into a new company called Dotmo to manage costs. Current Snap staff are leaving to focus exclusively on AI video development.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Key Talent Ahead of IPO
cards.body: OpenAI hired Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. The moves come as the company prepares for its upcoming IPO.

cards.title: Nearly Half of US Singles Skeptical of AI Dating
cards.body: About 47% of singles view AI negatively in dating apps, according to Match's survey. However, many users remain open to AI assistance with profile creation and conversation starters.

cards.title: Amazon Plans to Sell AI Chips Directly
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its AI chips to other data centers, challenging Nvidia's dominance. CEO Andy Jassy identifies this as a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

cards.title: FERC Fast-Tracks AI Data Center Grid Access
cards.body: Federal regulators told grid operators to prioritize data center interconnections in a new fast lane. The move fails to address underlying electricity supply shortages for AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Karamo Brown Launches AI Wellness App Kē
cards.body: The "Queer Eye" life coach created Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone. The app draws from his year-and-a-half personal journey across fitness, mental health, and relationships.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $300M at $2B Valuation
cards.body: General Intuition is in talks to raise $300 million for training embodied AI and world models. The startup leverages Medal's dataset of 2 billion videos per year from 10 million users.

cards.title: Tech Workers Launch $5M Political PAC
cards.body: Guardrails, a worker-backed PAC, is mounting a populist challenge to Big Tech's political influence with grassroots donations. The $5 million effort targets the AI industry's growing power.

cards.title: Pixi Brings AR Experiences to Text Messaging
cards.body: Pixi's new iOS app transforms text messages into interactive augmented reality experiences. The app positions itself as the next evolution beyond stickers, GIFs, and emoji reactions.

cards.title: How to Disable Gemini in Google Docs
cards.body: Google Docs users can now turn off AI features to eliminate "write with Gemini" pop-ups. The guide provides steps to customize AI assistance in the productivity tool.

cards.title: Roelof Botha Joins SpaceX Board
cards.body: Former Sequoia Capital leader Roelof Botha has joined SpaceX's board of directors. The appointment fills an existing vacancy days after SpaceX's historic IPO.

cards.title: Snap's AR Glasses Debut Disappoints Stock
cards.body: Snap unveiled expensive AR glasses, but the launch failed to boost investor confidence. The company's stock declined following the long-awaited hardware announcement.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Newest AI Models
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after researchers found ways to bypass guardrails. The ban hasn't stopped market momentum for the company.

cards.title: Reliance Weaves AI Into 500M+ Telecom Users
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI across telecom services, apps, and home devices for over 500 million people.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires DeductiveAI Bug-Catching Platform
cards.body: Elastic is buying AI startup DeductiveAI for up to $85M, which uses machine learning to detect and resolve software bugs. The three-year-old startup has caught attention in the debugging space.

cards.title: AI Inference Startup Baseten Raises $1.5B
cards.body: Baseten is finalizing a massive $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation, capitalizing on the AI inference boom. This comes months after the startup's previous mega-round.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team Into Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is launching Dotmo, a new company focused on AI video development, composed of current staff leaving the social platform. The spinoff reflects cost pressures on AI initiatives.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Transformer Co-Inventor Before IPO
cards.body: OpenAI is strengthening its leadership by bringing on Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. The moves signal preparations ahead of the company's planned IPO.

cards.title: Nearly Half of US Singles Reject AI Dating
cards.body: About 47% of American singles view AI negatively in dating apps, though many are open to AI for profile optimization. The finding reveals mixed consumer sentiment on AI romance tools.

cards.title: Amazon Sells Its AI Chips to Competitors
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its custom AI chips to other data centers as CEO Andy Jassy targets a $50 billion opportunity. The move challenges Nvidia's dominance in AI hardware.

cards.title: AI Data Centers Get Government Grid Fast Lane
cards.body: FERC granted AI data centers expedited interconnection to the electrical grid, but failed to address underlying electricity supply shortages. The mandate speeds deployment but sidesteps resource constraints.

cards.title: Karamo Brown Launches AI Wellness Clone App
cards.body: 'Queer Eye' coach Karamo Brown released Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone for fitness, meditation, and personal guidance. The app draws from his year-long wellness journey.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $300M for Embodied AI
cards.body: The startup is in talks to raise $300M at a $2B valuation, training embodied AI and world models using billions of video datasets. General Intuition leverages Medal's 2 billion annual videos from 10M users.

cards.title: How to Turn Off Google's AI in Docs
cards.body: Google Docs users can disable the 'write with Gemini' AI feature if unwanted prompts are appearing. A simple toggle removes the AI assistant from the interface.

cards.title: Subquadratic Claims LLM Bottleneck Breakthrough
cards.body: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic emerged from stealth claiming to solve a decade-old mathematical bottleneck limiting large language models. Skepticism remains pending technical validation.

cards.title: Brain-Computer Implants Enable ALS Patient Power User
cards.body: Casey Harrell, paralyzed by ALS, has used a brain-computer interface implant for nearly three years to communicate and control devices. The breakthrough demonstrates practical BCI applications beyond research.
